data.title: GATX beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• GATX press release (GATX): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.44 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $449M (+8.6% Y/Y) beats by $3.83M.
  • Rail North America’s fleet utilization remained strong at 99.0%; Lease Price Index (LPI) at 21.9%.
  • Full-year investment volume exceeded $1.3 billion.
  • The acquisition of Wells Fargo's rail operating lease portfolio was completed on Jan. 1, 2026.
  • Company initiates 2026 earnings guidance of $9.50–$10.10 per diluted share vs. consnesus of $9.99.

data.title: Newmont issues default notice to Barrick over Nevada JV mismanagement
data.description:
Top gold producer Newmont (NEM) issued a notice of default to rival Barrick Mining (B) after uncovering signs of alleged mismanagement at their Nevada joint venture, heightening tensions between the partners in a major mining area.

Barrick (B) allegedly diverted resources from the venture, Nevada Gold Mines, or NGM, to benefit its wholly-owned Fourmile project, Newmont (NEM) said in a filing to the US Securities and Exchange Commission on Thursday.

"On January 26, 2026, we informed Barrick and the NGM Board of Managers that we had identified evidence of mismanagement at NGM, including diversion of resources from NGM to the benefit of Barrick’s wholly-owned property Fourmile and Barrick, and that we were exercising our contractual inspection and audit rights," the filing said.

"On February 3, 2026, we sent Barrick a notice of default under the Nevada JV Agreement related to this conduct. Although we continue to work with Barrick to improve the performance of NGM and will take appropriate steps to address this matter, any such disagreements could have a material adverse effect on our interest in NGM, the business of NGM or the portion of our growth strategy related to NGM."

NGM is 38.5% owned by Newmont and Barrick holds a 61.5% economic interest.

Nevada Gold Mines' attributable gold production increased 17% to 293 thousand ounces in the fourth quarter, compared to the prior quarter, with a 1% increase in CAS per ounce to $1,258.

data.title: Inseego outlines $190M 2026 revenue target with expanded Tier 1 carrier partnerships and diversified product launches
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Inseego Corp. (INSG) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Juho Sarvikas stated that "Q4 2025 was another strong quarter for Inseego. We generated revenue of $48.4 million and adjusted EBITDA of $6 million, both above our guidance and marking our third consecutive quarter of sequential growth in each metric." He emphasized that these results reflect a "meaningfully higher quality and more diversified revenue base driven by broader product breadth and increased customer diversity."
  • Sarvikas detailed that Inseego "significantly expanded our Tier 1 carrier footprint for Fixed Wireless Access," securing an FX4200 FWA award with AT&T and announcing a new agreement with Verizon. All three major U.S. carriers have now chosen Inseego for their enterprise FWA offerings, which he called "an important inflection point for our business."
  • The CEO highlighted that the mobile hotspot portfolio "delivered its strongest quarter of 2025 with revenue increasing 27% sequentially to $20.4 million," noting that mobile represented approximately 40% of total company revenue in Q4.
  • Sarvikas explained, "For the first time, Inseego Connect is being taken to market alongside our FWA solutions by all 3 Tier 1 U.S. carriers," describing this as a shift from device-led to solution-led selling.
  • The company retired all preferred stock at a discount, welcoming Mubadala Capital as a significant common stockholder, which Sarvikas described as enhancing "the company's long-term flexibility."
  • CFO Steven Gatoff said, "On the top line, total revenue for Q4 was $48.4 million, driven by higher mobile volumes, increased channel activity, continued strength in FWA and a consistent contribution from our Inseego Connect and Inseego Subscribe SaaS offerings."
  • Gatoff commented, "Non-GAAP gross margin in Q4 2025 was 43%, up 75 basis points sequentially and driven by sales of some high-margin mobile products and the continued contribution from our high-margin SaaS services."

Outlook

  • The company is guiding for Q1 2026 total revenue in a range of $33 million to $36 million and adjusted EBITDA in a range of $1 million to $2 million.
  • For the full year 2026, Inseego is projecting total revenue of approximately $190 million.
  • Management stated, "This is a much more front-loaded year with a higher concentration of carrier launches and product introductions in the first half, specifically in Q1."
  • Sarvikas added, "We have more products going to more customers than this company has ever had. Q1 is a transition quarter, and there are several moving parts as we introduce a new mobile product generation and work with new large customers to develop joint go-to-market for FWA."

Financial Results

  • In Q4 2025, total revenue was $48.4 million, driven by strong mobile performance, expanded channel activity, sustained FWA results, and stable SaaS contributions.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 2025 was $6 million, with a 12.4% margin, which Gatoff highlighted as "among the highest dollars and margin percentage also in more than a decade."
  • The balance sheet at quarter-end included $24.9 million in cash and debt of $41 million, with Gatoff explaining that the "strong cash finish to the year was a function of a combination of favorable outcomes on customer payments, inventory dynamics and strong working capital management."
  • Software services revenue held steady at $12 million in Q4, providing a stable high-margin contribution.
  • The retirement of all preferred stock at a 38% discount reduced long-term obligations and improved balance sheet quality.

Q&A

  • Scott Searle, ROTH Capital: Asked about memory supply and the ramp-up expectations for 2026 given the ambitious revenue target. Juho Sarvikas responded, "from a memory standpoint...we realized somewhere around 6 months ago that the memory market is going to get tight. So we took the appropriate actions. So I feel good about the first half inventory situation as well as the pricing environment."
  • Searle inquired about competitive positioning and product expansion. Sarvikas explained, "we'll have now all 3 large carriers launching our new mobile generation...our job really is to go and consolidate the market. And these 3 across all 3 is a huge, huge milestone."
  • Tyler Burmeister, Lake Street: Asked if FWA will be a relatively larger contributor to growth in 2026. Gatoff answered, "We expect mobile and FWA both to grow on a revenue basis in 2026 for albeit different reasons."
  • Burmeister also asked about MSO and distribution channel growth. Sarvikas replied, "MSO is definitely something where I expect that we'll have great discussions as the year progresses. The VAR and the managed service provider...become significant growth driver for us, but will be a slower burn."
  • Christian Schwab, Craig-Hallum: Raised the potential for software expansion with other major carriers. Sarvikas indicated, "Inseego Connect is a really important part of our story. It also provides other service opportunities for us where we can expand."
  • Lance Vitanza, TD Cowen: Asked about the impact of Verizon's addition on business visibility and EBITDA margin. Gatoff responded, "the first part of the year is going to be a bit lower. So the average for the year is somewhere in between that's not really existing. But the short answer is the rates that we're at, we would expect to be seeing in the second half and exiting the year for sure."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on the feasibility of the aggressive 2026 revenue ramp, competitive positioning, and product launch execution; tone was generally positive but probing, especially regarding supply chain management and the sustainability of margin expansion.
  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, using phrases like "we feel really good" and emphasizing disciplined execution, while acknowledging short-term challenges and transition dynamics in Q1 2026.
  • Compared to the prior quarter, both analysts and management exhibited increased confidence, with management expressing greater certainty about product launches and diversified growth drivers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from a focus on building foundation and leadership expansion in Q3 2025 to executing at scale with multiple major product and carrier launches in Q4.
  • Strategic focus evolved from platform and software integration to full-scale multi-carrier product rollout and solution-based selling.
  • Management's tone in the current quarter was more assertive in projecting growth and financial targets, stating full-year revenue and EBITDA goals for 2026.
  • Key metric change: Q4 2025 revenue and EBITDA both increased sequentially, and the company retired preferred stock, strengthening the balance sheet.
  • Analysts' questions in Q4 centered more on execution risk, market share, and channel expansion, versus product development and foundational growth in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing memory market price increases and supply shortages, but noted proactive supply management and customer price negotiations to mitigate impact.
  • Q1 2026 is described as a transition quarter with lower sequential revenue due to engineering delays in new mobile products and inventory overhang at one Tier 1 FWA customer.
  • There is short-term disruption from a Tier 1 carrier's change in go-to-market strategy, expected to affect selling logistics but not long-term opportunity.
  • Analysts highlighted the challenge of executing an ambitious revenue and product ramp and maintaining margins amid increased investment.

Final Takeaway

Inseego exited 2025 with momentum, driven by a broadened Tier 1 carrier base, record sequential growth, and a shift toward solution-led enterprise wireless offerings. With all three major U.S. carriers launching new products and a robust 2026 revenue target of $190 million, management underscores confidence in scaling the business, despite a transitional first quarter and industry supply headwinds. The expanded platform and channel strategy, along with a strengthened balance sheet after retiring preferred stock, position the company to capitalize on accelerating enterprise adoption of fixed wireless and integrated connectivity solutions.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Valaris EPS of $10.26, revenue of $537.4M beats by $42.99M; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Valaris press release (VAL): Q4 EPS of $10.26 may not be comparable to consensus of $0.84.
  • Revenue of $537.4M (-8.0% Y/Y) beats by $42.99M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $97 million.

  • FY 2026 Financial Guidance

    • Total operating revenues of $2,125 - 2,205 million vs. consensus of $2.12B
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $485 - 565 million, which excludes costs associated with the pending business combination with Transocean Ltd. announced on February 9, 2026
    • Capital expenditures of $425 - 475 million
    • Upfront payments from customers related to contract-specific upgrades of approximately $110 million

data.title: U.S. December imports from Taiwan surpass Beijing as shipments plunge 44%
data.description:
• For the first time in decades, the United States imported more goods from Taiwan than from China, as President Donald Trump’s tariffs reshape trade flows.
  • US purchases of goods from China plunged almost 44% in December from a year earlier to $21.1 billion, Commerce Department data showed Thursday. By contrast, shipments from Taiwan more than doubled during the same period to $24.7 billion.
  • The deficit with China decreased $93.4 billion to $202.1 billion in 2025. Exports decreased $36.9 billion to $106.3 billion and imports decreased $130.4 billion to $308.4 billion.
  • The deficit with Taiwan meanwhile, increased $73.0 billion to $146.8 billion in 2025. Exports increased $12.1 billion to $54.7 billion and imports increased $85.2 billion to $201.4 billion.
  • Overall, U.S. trade deficit continued to widen in December after hitting the smallest monthly amount since the 2009 low in October.

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM GDP 

Gross Domestic Product represents the total value of the country's production during the period and consists of the purchases of domestically produced goods and services by individuals, businesses, foreigners, and government entities.

The initial estimate for Q4 is seen at a growth rate of 2.8 percent after 4.4 percent in Q3. PCE is expected at 2.4 percent after 3.5 percent in Q3. 

8:30 AM Personal Income and Outlays

Personal income represents the income that households receive from all sources, including wages and salaries, fringe benefits such as employer contributions to private pension plans, proprietors' income, income from rent, dividends and interest, and transfer payments such as Social Security and unemployment compensation. 

Spending is expected to be up 0.3 percent and income at 0.3 percent in the delayed report for December. Core PCE prices are expected to be up 0.3 percent, an unpleasant outcome for anyone hoping for more aggressive rate cuts.

9:45 AM PMI Composite Flash

The flash Composite Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) provides an early estimate of current private sector output by combining information obtained from surveys of around 1,000 manufacturing and service sector companies.

Manufacturing PMI is seen at 51.9 in February flash versus 52.4 in the January final. 

9:45 AM Raphael Bostic Speaks

Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic participates in a moderated conversation on the economic outlook before the event hosted by the Birmingham Business Journal. 

10:00 AM New Home Sales

New home sales measure the number of newly constructed homes with a committed sale during the month.

The delayed reports are expected to show sales at an annual 735K rate in November and 728K rate in December.

10:00 AM Consumer Sentiment

The University of Michigan's Consumer Survey Center questions households each month on their assessment of current conditions and expectations of future conditions.

The final February report is expected to show the sentiment index at 57.0 versus 57.3 in the preliminary February report compared with 56.4 in the January final and a much stronger 64.7 a year ago.

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count 

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs.

3:30 PM Alberto Musalem Speaks 

St. Louis Federal Reserve Bank President Alberto Musalem appears on Fox Business.

data.title: Forum Energy beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Forum Energy press release (FET): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $202.2M (+0.6% Y/Y) beats by $11.70M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA: $23 million
  • Operating cash flow and free cash flow: $22 million and $22 million, respectively.
  • Orders: $891 million, book-to-bill ratio of 113%.

  • Full Year 2026 Guidance (comparisons at midpoint)

    • Revenue: $800 - $880 million, up 6% vs. consensus of $802.90M
    • Adjusted net income: $18 - $38 million, up $21 million
    • Adjusted EBITDA: $90 - $110 million, a 16% increase
    • Free cash flow: $55 - $75 million, 65% free cash flow conversion

data.title: Lundin Mining beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; sees copper production 2026 forecast to remain stable
data.description:
• Lundin Mining press release (LUNMF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.42 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $1.35B (+62.0% Y/Y) beats by $140M, comprised of $1,301.5 million from continuing operations with a realized copper price3 of $5.89 /lb and a realized gold price3 of $4,412 /oz, and $52.2 million from discontinued operations4.
  • Copper Production2: Consolidated copper production of 87,032 tonnes at a consolidated copper cash cost3 of $1.88 /lb.
  • Other Production2: During the quarter, 34,129 ounces of gold and 2,174 tonnes of nickel were produced.
  • Adjusted EBITDA3: $700.6 million for the quarter, $686.4 million generated from continuing operations and $14.2 million generated from discontinued operations.
  • Free cash flow from operations3 was $412.5 million, $388.3 million from continuing operations and $24.2 million from discontinued operations, which included a working capital build of $132.1 million from continuing operations.
  • Net cash3: As at December 31, the net cash position of the Company was $77.4 million which includes $22.0 million cash from discontinued operations.
  • Outlook: Copper production is forecast to remain stable at approximately 310,000 – 335,000 tonnes annually in 2026, consistent with 2025 production. Consolidated cash cost in 2026 is forecast to be within $1.90/lb to $2.10/lb of copper which is in line with 2025 guidance. As part of the Company's full potential programs, the focus will continue to be on cost reductions and process improvements.

data.title: Centerra Gold Non-GAAP EPS of $0.41, revenue of $401.6M; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Centerra Gold press release (CGAU): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.41.
  • Revenue of $401.6M (+32.8% Y/Y).
  • In the fourth quarter 2025, consolidated gold production was 70,853 ounces, including 44,105 ounces from the Mount Milligan Mine (“Mount Milligan”) and 26,748 ounces from the Öksüt Mine (“Öksüt”). Copper production in the quarter was 13.0 million pounds.
  • Fourth quarter 2025 gold sales were 68,143 ounces at an average realized gold price of $3,415 per ounce and copper sales were 12.5 million pounds at an average realized copper price of $4.69 per pound. 
  • Fourth quarter 2025 consolidated gold production costs were $1,259 per ounce and all-in sustaining costs (“AISC”) on a by-product basisNG were $1,646 per ounce.
  •  Fourth quarter 2025 additions to property, plant, and equipment (“PP&E”) and capital expendituresNG were $115.2 million and $96.0 million, respectively.

  • 2026 Guidance Highlights

    • Production: In 2026, consolidated gold production is expected to be 250,000 to 280,000 ounces. Copper production in 2026 is expected to be 50 to 60 million pounds.
    • Costs: In 2026, consolidated gold production costs are expected to be $1,500 to $1,600 per ounce and AISC on a by-product basisNG is expected to be $1,650 to $1,750 per ounce.
    • Capital ExpendituresNG: In 2026, sustaining capital expendituresNG are expected to be $85 to $105 million.
    • Molybdenum Roasting: Following the incident that occurred at Langeloth in late January 2026, Centerra continues to assess the full operational and financial impacts, and will provide 2026 operating guidance for Langeloth at a later date.
